questions.question: What is the primary goal of boundary following in morphological image processing?
options: To remove noise from an image
To enhance image contrast
To track the boundaries of objects in an image
To apply morphological operations to the entire image
questions.explanation: Boundary following is a technique used to track the boundaries of objects in an image by iteratively applying morphological operations to the image.

questions.question: What is the primary advantage of using chain codes in boundary preprocessing?
options: They provide detailed information about the shape of objects in an image
They are faster to compute than other boundary representation methods
They are more suitable for binary images
They are used in conjunction with other boundary representation methods
questions.explanation: Chain codes are a compact representation of the boundary of an object in an image, providing detailed information about the shape of the object.

questions.question: What is the main difference between chain codes and boundary following?
options: Chain codes are used for binary images, while boundary following is used for grayscale images
Chain codes represent the boundary of an object as a sequence of turns and ends, while boundary following iteratively applies morphological operations to the image
Chain codes are faster to compute than boundary following
Chain codes are used for 3D images, while boundary following is used for 2D images
questions.explanation: Chain codes represent the boundary of an object as a sequence of turns and ends, while boundary following iteratively applies morphological operations to the image.

questions.question: What is the primary application of chain codes in computer vision?
options: Object recognition
Image segmentation
Boundary detection
Image filtering
questions.explanation: Chain codes are commonly used in boundary detection applications, such as detecting edges and contours in images.

questions.question: What is the primary limitation of chain codes in boundary preprocessing?
options: They can only be used for binary images
They can only represent simple shapes
They can be computationally expensive to compute
They can be sensitive to noise in the image
questions.explanation: Chain codes can be computationally expensive to compute, especially for complex shapes, which can be a limitation in certain applications.
